name: King Cobra
description: Group of venomous snakes from Asia
summary: The king cobra is a species complex of snakes endemic to Asia. With an average length of 3.18 to 4 m and a record length of 5.85 m (19.2 ft), it is the world's longest venomous snake and among the heaviest. Under the genus Ophiophagus, it is not phylogenetically a true cobra despite its common name and some resemblance. Spanning from the Indian subcontinent through Southeastern Asia to Southern China, the king cobra is widely distributed albeit not commonly seen.

wikipedia_sections.etymology: Under the genus Ophiophagus , it is not phylogenetically a true cobra despite its common name and some resemblance. Hamadryas hannah was the scientific name used by Danish naturalist Theodore Edward Cantor in 1836 who described four king cobra specimens , three captured in the Sundarbans and one in the vicinity of Kolkata . 5 Taxonomy The genus Ophiophagus was proposed by Günther in 1864 in place of Hamadryas , as the genus Hamadryas was already used for the cracker butterflies . 6 The name is derived from its propensity to eat snakes.
wikipedia_sections.description: With an average length of 3.18 to 4 m (10.4 to 13.1 ft) and a record length of 5.85 m (19.2 ft), 2 it is the world's longest venomous snake and among the heaviest. Individuals have diversified colouration across its habitats , from black with white strips to unbroken brownish grey, although after taxonomic re-evaluation, it is no longer the sole member of its genus but is now a species complex; these differences in pattern and other aspects may cause the genus to be split into at least four species, spread across its large geographic range .
wikipedia_sections.distribution: Not to be confused with Indian cobra . Spanning from the Indian subcontinent through Southeastern Asia to Southern China , the king cobra is widely distributed albeit not commonly seen. Capable of striking at a considerable range and height with an immense venom yield, envenomation from this species may induce rapid onset of neurotoxic and cytotoxic symptoms, requiring prompt antivenom administration. wikipedia_sections.habitat: These populations have sometimes been thought to be the result of introduction by snake charmers or transport along rivers, but are now more likely considered natural populations. 66 67 Relationship with humans Conservation In Southeast Asia, the king cobra is threatened foremost by habitat destruction owing to deforestation and expansion of agricultural land. Tropical Conservation Science . "The Hamadryad or King Cobra Naja hannah (Cantor)" . The Journal of the Bombay Natural History Society . ^ Bhaisare, D.; Ramanuj, V.; Shankar, P.
wikipedia_sections.hunting_diet: It chiefly hunts other snakes , including those of its own kind , although other lizards and rodents are occasional prey items. 2 Following envenomation , it swallows its prey whole. It is considered diurnal because it hunts during the day, but has also been seen at night, albeit rarely. 2 Diet Preying on a smaller snake The king cobra is an apex predator and dominant over all other snakes except large pythons . 33 It also hunts Malabar pit viper and hump-nosed pit viper by following their odour trails.
wikipedia_sections.behavior: 5 Behaviour and ecology Like other snakes, a king cobra receives chemical information via its forked tongue , which picks up scent particles and transfers them to a sensory receptor ( Jacobson's organ ) located in the roof of its mouth. They may be brightly marked, but these colours often fade as they mature. They are alert and nervous, being highly aggressive if disturbed. 22 The average lifespan of a wild king cobra is about 20 years. How Snakes Work: Structure, Function and Behavior of the World's Snakes .
wikipedia_sections.reproduction: This is the only ophidian that constructs an above-ground nest for its eggs, which are purposefully and meticulously gathered and protected by the female throughout the incubation period. 41 Reproduction A captive juvenile king cobra in its defensive posture The female is gravid for 50 to 59 days. 18 The king cobra is the only snake that builds a nest using dry leaf litter , starting from late March to late May. They consist of several layers and have mostly one chamber, into which the female lays eggs.
wikipedia_sections.threats: King cobra Coiled juvenile Vulnerable ( 3.1 ) 1 II 1 Animalia Chordata Reptilia Squamata Suborder: Serpentes Elapidae Ophiophagus Günther , 1864 O. Threatened by habitat destruction , it has been listed as Vulnerable on the Red List since 2010. It is also threatened by wildlife smuggling , as well as by poaching , then sold as bushmeat or turned into snake leather, and for use in traditional Chinese medicine . 1 The king cobra is listed in II .
